findings.requirement: US-16: Warning text for delayacct-only unavailable case
findings.detail: User-spec Scenario 2 (line 45) quotes the combined-unavailable warning string ('iodelay and IO stats unavailable: task_delayacct=0; run: sysctl -w kernel.task_delayacct=1, then re-open screen'). Tech-spec Decision 5 correctly separates combined vs. delayacct-only messages but does not derive the exact delayacct-only wording from user-spec — it uses 'iodelay unavailable (task_delayacct=0): ...' instead. The split itself is correctly specified in user-spec prose ('если только delayacct — только про delayacct'), but the exact delayacct-only message text is implicit in user-spec, leaving a minor wording ambiguity.

summary: 22/23 requirements covered, 1 partial. The tech-spec provides thorough coverage of all user-spec requirements: 19-column layout, new iodelay columns with correct formulas, availability probe, first-tick and PID-gone boundary cases, old-kernel guard, warning mutual-exclusion logic, no-cpuCount-normalization rationale, reuse of existing helpers, unit test list, and documentation tasks. One minor partial: the exact warning text for the delayacct-only case is not verbatim from user-spec (user-spec implies it but does not state it explicitly for the single-probe case). No scope creep, no YAGNI violations, no missing error handling, no structural gaps, no shallow solution.
